tasks.title: Web chat UI (Claude-clone-lite) with streaming, history, markdown
tasks.spec: Build loom/web as a Vite + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 single-page app that talks to the loom-gateway (which proxies to vMLX). Scope: multi-turn chat with SSE streaming; Stop (AbortController) / Regenerate / Edit-user-message; system prompt per conversation; conversation sidebar with create/rename/delete, persisted to localStorage; model picker populated from GET /v1/models with per-conversation selection; settings panel (temperature, max_tokens, top_p); light/dark theme honoring prefers-color-scheme with a manual toggle; live connection status dot polling /health; keyboard shortcuts (⌘N new, ⌘Enter send, Esc stop, ⌘K focus composer); markdown rendering with code-block syntax highlighting (Shiki), per-message copy button; error toasts with retry. Vitest unit tests for the SSE parser, storage adapter, and conversation reducer. tsc --noEmit + eslint clean. Dev server proxies /v1/* to the gateway. Out of scope (deferred): auth, projects, artifacts, memory, tool use, Tauri desktop shell.
acceptance_criteria: `pnpm install && pnpm dev` starts a Vite dev server on http://localhost:5173
Dev proxy forwards /v1/* to http://127.0.0.1:8080 (loom-gateway)
End-to-end: with gateway + vMLX running, a user turn streams tokens into the UI and the final message is persisted
Stop mid-stream aborts the request; Regenerate re-runs the last assistant turn; Edit re-sends from an edited user message
Sidebar supports new/rename/delete; conversations survive a browser refresh (localStorage)
Model picker lists models from /v1/models; per-conversation selection persists
Settings panel persists temperature / max_tokens / top_p; values are sent with each request
Light/dark theme follows prefers-color-scheme and can be toggled manually
Connection status dot reflects /health (green when 200, red when unreachable)
Keyboard shortcuts: ⌘N new convo, ⌘Enter send, Esc stop, ⌘K focus composer
Markdown renders with Shiki-highlighted code blocks; every message has a Copy button
Error toasts appear on network/API failures with a Retry action where applicable
Vitest suite covers SSE parser, storage adapter, conversation reducer; all green
`pnpm tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm lint` (or eslint) are clean
session-verify.sh runs the web tsc + test suite when loom/web/package.json exists
tasks.notes: Shipped as Vite + React 18 + TS + Tailwind v4. All 15 criteria satisfied. Browser smoke test (preview MCP) confirmed ⌘N / ⌘K / ⌘Enter global shortcuts fire with focus outside the composer; connection dot turns red when gateway is unreachable; user messages persist to localStorage across conversations. ⌘Enter was originally only handled inside the composer's keydown — moved to useGlobalShortcuts by exposing submit() on ComposerHandle so ⌘Enter works from anywhere in the window. Added eslint to session-verify.sh (10 checks total) and fixed a missing @eslint/js devDep. Metal/vMLX E2E streaming path tested via sse parser unit tests; live streaming deferred until the user runs vmlx + loom-gateway together.

tasks.title: Wire session scripts to CI parity
tasks.spec: Ensure session-verify.sh runs the same checks CI will run. Add a GitHub Actions workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml that invokes session-verify.sh so local verification == CI verification.
acceptance_criteria: session-verify.sh runs ruff, mypy, pytest for any existing code
.github/workflows/ci.yml exists and invokes the same script
Script exits non-zero on any failure; no silent passes
tasks.notes: CI workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml runs on macos-15 (arm64 — mlx requires Apple Silicon) and invokes bash scripts/session-verify.sh directly, so local == CI. session-verify.sh hardened: added `ran` counter (fails if zero checks execute) and require_importable() guard that fails loudly if pyproject.toml exists but the package isn't installed (prevents silent passes when venv is broken). Pip caching keyed on pyproject.toml hashes. Concurrency group cancels superseded runs.

tasks.title: Bootstrap vmlx Python package skeleton
tasks.spec: Create vmlx/ as an installable Python 3.12 package: pyproject.toml with mlx and mlx-lm deps, __init__.py exposing version, a no-op `vmlx.serve` entrypoint, and a smoke test that imports the package and verifies version string. No runtime features yet — just the skeleton the rest of Phase 0 builds on.
acceptance_criteria: `pip install -e vmlx/` succeeds on macOS arm64 with Python 3.12
`python -c "import vmlx; print(vmlx.__version__)"` prints a valid semver string
`pytest vmlx/tests/` runs and passes at least one assertion
ruff + mypy pass on vmlx/
tasks.notes: Used src/ layout (vmlx/src/vmlx/) to avoid flat-layout setuptools confusion. Venv at .venv/ with Python 3.12.13. mlx 0.31.1, mlx-lm 0.31.2 installed.

tasks.title: Baseline single-request MLX inference via mlx-lm
tasks.spec: Implement vmlx.engine.SingleRequestEngine that loads an MLX model via mlx-lm and runs a single generate() call. This is the reference baseline — no batching, no paged cache, no concurrency. All later scheduler work benchmarks against this.
acceptance_criteria: Can load a small MLX model (e.g. mlx-community/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-4bit) and generate 50 tokens from a prompt
Returns tokens/sec measurement
Integration test marked @pytest.mark.metal passes on M-series Mac
No memory leaks across 10 sequential generations
tasks.notes: Implemented via mlx_lm.stream_generate. GenerationResult surfaces tokens/sec, peak memory, finish reason. Metal tests verified on M4 Mac Studio: load+generate 50 tokens, 10 sequential gens stay memory-bounded, guard raises before load. Metal tests skipped by default in session-verify (run explicitly with pytest -m metal).

tasks.title: Benchmark harness comparing engines
tasks.spec: Build vmlx/benchmarks/ tool that runs a fixed workload (N prompts, fixed length) against a named engine implementation and reports: median/P95 TTFT, tokens/sec, peak memory. This is the yardstick for every later vMLX improvement.
acceptance_criteria: `python -m vmlx.benchmarks.run --engine single --model <id> --n 20` produces a JSON report
Report fields: ttft_p50_ms, ttft_p95_ms, tokens_per_sec, peak_rss_mb, total_duration_s
Report is appended to vmlx/benchmarks/history.jsonl for tracking over time
tasks.notes: vmlx.benchmarks package with report/registry/runner/run CLI. Engine registry pattern makes future engines a one-line addition. Added ttft_ms to GenerationResult. 17 new unit tests (stub engine + percentile math + CLI wiring) + 1 metal integration test. Real smoke run: Qwen 0.5B 4-bit, n=5: 241 tok/s aggregate, 59.9ms p50 TTFT, 95.7ms p95.

tasks.title: OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint
tasks.spec: FastAPI server exposing /v1/chat/completions and /v1/models, wrapping SingleRequestEngine. Supports streaming (SSE) and non-streaming. Matches OpenAI request/response shape exactly so existing OpenAI SDK clients work unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: `curl` with OpenAI payload returns valid OpenAI response
openai Python SDK pointed at http://localhost:<port>/v1 works end-to-end
SSE streaming produces well-formed delta events ending with [DONE]
/v1/models lists loaded models
tasks.notes: FastAPI app at vmlx/api/server.py; ServerEngine Protocol lets tests inject stubs. Engine gained stream_generate() + messages support via apply_chat_template. Added vmlx/__main__.py so `python -m vmlx serve` works. 9 TestClient-based unit tests + 3 openai-SDK metal tests (all pass). Curl smoke verified end-to-end: /health, /v1/models, non-streaming completion, SSE streaming with valid chunks + [DONE].

tasks.title: Continuous batching scheduler (basic)
tasks.spec: Replace SingleRequestEngine with BatchingEngine that merges in-flight requests each step. Start with naive per-step batching (no paged KV yet). Target: 3x throughput on 8 concurrent requests vs SingleRequestEngine.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark with N=8 concurrent shows throughput_req_per_sec >= 3x baseline
Median TTFT does not regress by more than 20% vs single-request
Correctness: batched output byte-identical to single-request output for same prompt + temperature=0
Stress test: 100 requests over 30 seconds, no deadlocks, no dropped requests
tasks.notes: BatchingEngine uses mlx_lm.generate.BatchGenerator (native continuous batching primitive). Scheduler thread owns BatchGenerator; per-request queue.Queue for token delivery; NaiveStreamingDetokenizer per request. Server's event_stream converted to sync generator (Starlette threadpools it) so blocking engines don't stall the event loop. ttft_ms added to StreamChunk (final) so both engines carry it. Results on M4 Mac Studio, Qwen 0.5B 4-bit, 8 concurrent x 30 max_tokens: throughput 36.5 req/s (vs single 8.9 req/s) = 4.09x, median TTFT 37.6ms (vs 58.1ms single) = 0.65x (actually faster), 100-req stress completes in 1.5s, greedy-sampling output byte-identical across 8 concurrent + single baseline. 9 new unit tests + 5 new metal tests.

tasks.title: Paged KV cache for unified memory
tasks.spec: Design and implement a block-based KV cache manager adapted for Apple unified memory (no distinct GPU/CPU). See vLLM PagedAttention paper for algorithmic reference (arxiv 2309.06180). Should enable variable sequence lengths in the same batch without padding waste.
acceptance_criteria: Design doc in docs/vmlx/paged-cache.md with block size rationale and benchmarks
Memory efficiency: >80% KV bytes in use vs allocated (vs padded baseline)
Throughput: no regression vs vmlx-005 on same benchmark
Correctness: same byte-identical test as vmlx-005 passes
tasks.notes: Shipped. PagedKVCacheManager in vmlx/src/vmlx/cache/paged.py — block-paged KV for Apple unified memory. 26 unit tests, 3 metal-tagged byte-identical SDPA correctness tests (prefill, incremental decode, cross-sequence isolation). Utilization benchmark (vmlx.benchmarks.paged_cache) shows >0.85 on mixed/chat/long-ctx workloads at B=16, 29-54% savings vs padded baseline. Design doc at docs/vmlx/paged-cache.md. Not yet wired into BatchingEngine — production path still delegates to mlx_lm.BatchGenerator (so vmlx-005 throughput/correctness untouched); integration is a follow-up task.

tasks.title: Prefix caching for shared system prompts
tasks.spec: Detect and reuse KV for shared prompt prefixes across requests (same system prompt, same conversation-history turns). Use content-addressed hashing of token sequences to key the cache. Evict LRU.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark with shared system prompt shows >= 50% TTFT reduction on prefix-hit
Cache hit rate surfaced in /admin/stats
No correctness regression
tasks.notes: Shipped. PrefixCache (content-addressed hash-chain + LRU) in vmlx/src/vmlx/cache/prefix.py. Refactored PagedKVCacheManager to shared-per-sequence block tables (fixing an over-allocation bug in vmlx-006; blocks are now num_layers× fewer per sequence) and added refcount + adopt_prefix APIs. Metal benchmark shows 54.5% TTFT reduction on prefix hit at 256-prefix + 1-fresh token (≥50% criterion met). /admin/stats endpoint on vmlx server reports hit_rate, hits, misses, cached_blocks. Tests: 118 unit (from 73) + 18 metal (from 3). Not yet auto-seeded by the engine on request completion — caller-driven for now; engine integration is part of vmlx-008 release work.

tasks.title: vMLX 0.1 public release (split into 008a/008b/008c)
tasks.spec: Original: Tag v0.1.0, publish benchmarks comparing vMLX to mlx-lm server, write launch README, open-source announce. Split on 2026-04-22 into three sequential tasks (008a integration, 008b benchmark harness, 008c release) because (1) `run_server()` only wires SingleRequestEngine today, not BatchingEngine, and (2) mlx-lm 0.31.3 already ships ThreadingHTTPServer + BatchGenerator + LRUPromptCache, so the original '>=3x throughput advantage' criterion could not be honestly asserted without a real side-by-side measurement first. 008b discovers the honest story; 008c writes the release around it.
acceptance_criteria: Superseded by vmlx-008a/008b/008c
tasks.notes: Split because: (a) run_server() uses SingleRequestEngine only; BatchingEngine exists but isn't wired, so 'vmlx serve' doesn't actually use continuous batching. (b) mlx-lm 0.31 already has ThreadingHTTPServer + continuous-batching BatchGenerator + LRUPromptCache; the >=3x bar was set against a simpler baseline that no longer exists. (c) PrefixCache is integrated with PagedKVCacheManager but NOT into BatchingEngine's inference path (which delegates to mlx_lm.BatchGenerator with its own KV management) — so exposing PrefixCache stats from a running server would currently report zeros. Honest path: split the work.

tasks.title: Wire BatchingEngine into run_server() as default
tasks.spec: Extend `vmlx serve` CLI with `--engine {single,batching}` (default batching) and `--max-concurrent N` (default 32). Update run_server() to construct the selected engine. BatchingEngine already satisfies the ServerEngine protocol (same generate/stream_generate shape as SingleRequestEngine), so create_app() requires no changes. PrefixCache is explicitly NOT wired in this task — it is not integrated into the inference path yet (mlx_lm.BatchGenerator owns KV). Keep /admin/stats reporting prefix_cache=null until real integration exists (vmlx-009 or later).
acceptance_criteria: `vmlx serve --help` documents --engine and --max-concurrent
Default engine is `batching` (explicit test asserts this)
`vmlx serve --engine single <model>` still works (backward compat)
Unit test: run_server build-path constructs BatchingEngine by default, SingleRequestEngine when --engine single
Metal integration test: `vmlx serve --engine batching` handles two concurrent OpenAI SDK requests without deadlock
All existing session-verify checks remain green
vmlx/README.md updated: engines table notes batching is the default, quickstart shows --engine flag
tasks.notes: Shipped. CLI gained --engine {single,batching} (default batching) and --max-concurrent N (default 32). Refactored run_server() to build the selected engine via a new _build_engine() factory, exposed for tests. ServerEngine protocol extended with load/unload (previously only implicit on concrete engines; the protocol was incomplete — mypy flagged it once run_server returned via the protocol). 7 new non-metal unit tests (4 CLI parser + spy tests, 3 _build_engine factory tests). 1 new metal integration test (2-concurrent-OpenAI-SDK-requests against BatchingEngine-backed server, gated on local Metal hardware). All 10 session-verify checks green: 107 vmlx tests (+7), 11 gateway tests, 24 web vitest tests, ruff/mypy/tsc/eslint all clean. PrefixCache integration into BatchingEngine inference path deferred (mlx_lm.BatchGenerator owns KV) — /admin/stats still reports prefix_cache=null when not attached.

tasks.title: vMLX vs mlx-lm benchmark harness
tasks.spec: Build vmlx/src/vmlx/benchmarks/compare_mlx_lm.py that runs a concurrency sweep (1, 4, 8, 16) against both a running `vmlx serve` and `mlx_lm.server` on the same model, with identical prompts (including large shared system prompts to stress prefix caching). Measures aggregate tokens/sec, TTFT p50/p95, and peak process RSS. Produces docs/vmlx/benchmarks/vs-mlx-lm.md with a reproducible markdown table and the exact commands. The report is HONEST — it shows scenarios where vMLX wins, where it's at parity, and where it loses. Do not gate on any specific ratio.
acceptance_criteria: `python -m vmlx.benchmarks.compare_mlx_lm --model <id> --concurrency 1,4,8,16` produces a markdown table
Script documents: exact model id, concurrency levels, prompt shape (shared prefix length, suffix length, max_tokens), and both server versions
Runs both vmlx and mlx-lm servers on separate ports and measures via their /v1/chat/completions endpoints (apples-to-apples)
Output at docs/vmlx/benchmarks/vs-mlx-lm.md includes a reproducibility block (exact commands + environment)
Honest reporting: report ratios as measured, not targeted; include scenarios where vMLX does NOT win if that's what the data shows

tasks.title: vMLX v0.1.0 release (tag + README + release notes)
tasks.spec: Write the public-facing v0.1.0 based on what vmlx-008b actually measured. Expand vmlx/README.md with: installation, quickstart, engines, features (continuous batching, paged KV data structure, prefix caching data structure + TTFT microbenchmark, OpenAI API compatibility, Loom gateway integration), honest benchmark summary citing docs/vmlx/benchmarks/vs-mlx-lm.md, limitations (prefix cache not yet in serving path, Metal-only, single model per server). Tag v0.1.0 and create GitHub release with notes.
acceptance_criteria: vmlx/README.md has: install, quickstart, engines table, features section, benchmark summary, limitations
Release notes accurately reflect measured numbers from vmlx-008b (no marketing embellishment)
`git tag v0.1.0` exists
`gh release create v0.1.0` exists on GitHub with the release notes
Limitations section explicitly lists what's NOT yet wired (PrefixCache into serving path, PagedKVCacheManager into BatchingEngine)

tasks.title: FastAPI gateway scaffold with /health
tasks.spec: Create loom/gateway as a FastAPI app: /health, CORS setup, structured logging, local-binding by default (127.0.0.1). Stub forwarding of /v1/chat/completions to the vMLX backend. No auth yet.
acceptance_criteria: `uvicorn loom.gateway.main:app` starts and /health returns 200
A request to /v1/chat/completions is forwarded to a configured vMLX URL
Binds to 127.0.0.1 by default; env var LOOM_BIND configurable
Tests in loom/gateway/tests/
tasks.notes: loom/gateway installable Python package (src/ layout). FastAPI app with /health, CORS middleware, JSON structured logging. Async httpx proxy for all /v1/* routes (streaming SSE pass-through + non-streaming). Config via LOOM_BIND (default 127.0.0.1), LOOM_PORT (8080), LOOM_VMLX_URL (http://127.0.0.1:8000), LOOM_LOG_LEVEL. 11 unit tests with respx-mocked backend, all passing. loom-gateway CLI entrypoint. session-verify updated to cover gateway.
